cards.back: GitOps continuous delivery tool for Kubernetes — watches a Git repo and syncs desired state to the cluster automatically
Git is the single source of truth — all changes go through Git, the cluster is always reconciled to match Git state
CRD that defines: source repo + path, destination cluster + namespace, sync policy. The unit of deployment in ArgoCD
Pattern where one root Application manages a directory of child Application manifests — adding a file to Git creates a new app automatically
Synced (cluster matches Git), OutOfSync (drift detected), Unknown (can't compare)
Healthy, Progressing, Degraded, Suspended, Missing, Unknown — based on resource health checks
ArgoCD automatically reverts manual kubectl changes back to Git state. Prevents configuration drift
ArgoCD deletes resources that exist in the cluster but are no longer in Git. Keeps cluster clean
Automated: ArgoCD syncs on every Git change. Manual: you trigger sync explicitly via UI/CLI/API
Controls deployment order — wave 0 deploys first (CRDs, namespaces), higher waves deploy after lower waves are healthy

cards.front: How does ArgoCD manage multiple clusters?
cards.back: Single ArgoCD control plane registers external clusters via argocd cluster add — stores kubeconfig as a secret in argocd namespace
Generate multiple Applications from a template — cluster generator, git generator, matrix generator. Scales to 100s of apps/clusters
Creates one Application per registered cluster — useful for deploying the same app to all clusters automatically
Creates one Application per directory or file in a Git repo — used in App of Apps pattern
Managed ArgoCD by the creators (Intuit/Akuity). Uses agent-based multi-cluster model — agents deployed per cluster, report to central control plane
Manually trigger a sync: argocd app sync <appname> --timeout 120
Show what would change if you synced — compares live cluster state vs Git desired state
Wait for app to reach a state: argocd app wait <appname> --health --timeout 120

cards.front: How to force refresh from Git?
cards.back: kubectl patch application <name> -n argocd --type merge -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"argocd.argoproj.io/refresh":"hard"}}}'
Runs argocd-server over HTTP instead of HTTPS — used when TLS is terminated externally by Traefik/nginx
kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d
argocd-server (UI+API), application-controller (reconciler), repo-server (renders manifests), redis (cache), dex (SSO), applicationset-controller

cards.front: How to handle secrets in GitOps?
cards.back: Never store plaintext in Git. Options: External Secrets Operator (pulls from AWS Secrets Manager), Sealed Secrets (encrypted in Git), SOPS
Syncs secrets from external stores (AWS Secrets Manager, Vault) into Kubernetes Secrets automatically
Encrypts K8s Secrets with a cluster key — safe to store in Git. Only the cluster can decrypt
